items.title: Financial risk management platform Pillar raises $20M seed in round led by a16z
items.description: Pillar's investors include a16z, Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi, Crucible Capital and Gallery Ventures.

items.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
items.description: The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it. There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

items.title: Google brings its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature to India
items.description: The feature lets users connect Google accounts like Gmail and Photos to get personalized answers.

items.title: Adobe fixes PDF zero-day security bug that hackers have exploited for months
items.description: It's not clear how many people were compromised by this hacking campaign, but a security researcher said the hackers were targeting victims since at least November 2025.

items.title: Tesla adds ‘streaks,’ other stats to track how often drivers use Full Self-Driving software
items.description: Tesla has also made it easy to subscribe to the advanced driver assistance system.

items.title: Instacart acquires Instaleap to expand its enterprise platform internationally
items.description: The acquisition will allow the company to expand its business into international markets without needing to launch a delivery network in those regions.

items.title: How vibe-coding app Anything is rebuilding after getting booted from the App Store twice
items.description: Anything is planning to launch a desktop companion app to aid mobile app development after its App Store removal.

items.title: Amazon to buy Globalstar for $11.57B in bid to flesh out its satellite biz
items.description: Amazon on Tuesday agreed to buy satellite company Globalstar, known for powering Apple's "Emergency SOS" feature, for $11.57 billion in cash.

items.title: Lucid Motors names new CEO, lands more money from Uber and Saudis
items.description: The search for a new CEO, which lasted over a year following the sudden resignation of Peter Rawlinson, is finally over.

items.title: Inertia moves to commercialize one of the world’s most elaborate science experiments
items.description: Inertia has signed three agreements with the Lawrence Livermore National Lab, paving the way for the company to bring its pioneering fusion reactor to market.

items.title: An Amazon warehouse worker died on the job at Oregon facility
items.description: Though Amazon has faced issues with warehouse safety, the company said that the death was not work related.

items.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
items.description: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.

items.title: Uber and Nuro begin testing premium robotaxi service in San Francisco
items.description: Uber employees can now hail a Lucid robotaxi as part of the testing.

items.title: Microsoft is officially killing its Outlook Lite app next month
items.description: Launched in 2022, Outlook Lite is a lightweight version of the regular Outlook app, designed for Android phones with limited storage and regions with slower internet connections. The app had already been scheduled for retirement.

items.title: Thousands of rare concert recordings are landing on the Internet Archive — listen now
items.description: Chicago-based music superfan Aadam Jacobs has been recording the concerts he goes to since the 1980s, amassing an archive of over 10,000 tapes.

items.title: IBM pays $17M fine to end DOJ suit over DEI programs
items.description: IBM entered into a $17 million settlement agreement on Friday with the U.S. DOJ over allegations that it engaged in “illegal DEI practices.”

items.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
items.description: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

items.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
items.description: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.

items.title: FBI announces takedown of phishing operation that targeted thousands of victims
items.description: Cybercriminals allegedly used the W3LL phishing kit to target more than 17,000 victims worldwide, stealing their passwords and multi-factor authentication codes.

items.title: Booking.com confirms hackers accessed customers’ data
items.description: The travel giant notified customers that their personal data, including names, email addresses, and phone numbers, may have been accessed in a security incident.
